texto: A great many grown-up and intelligent people believe, or pretend to believe, that by behaving in a friendly and accommodating way to our attackers, we will show them that they have nothing to fear from us and so defuse their wrath. The idea that such behavior would be taken by a ruthless and implacable enemy only as a sign of weakness is as foreign to them as the idea of honor itself.
explicacao: Citação de James Bowman sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “A great many grown-up and intelligent people believe, or pretend to believe, that by behaving in …” articula amizade, honor, weakness em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como termino e weakness.
